upcomingBills.title: American Broadband Deployment Act of 2026
upcomingBills.summary: Strips most environmental (NEPA) and historic preservation review requirements from wireless tower modifications and cell antenna upgrades. Auto-approves applications if government deadlines are missed. Limits local zoning authority over 5G small cell placement on utility poles, light poles, and buildings including schools.
upcomingBills.passReasoning: Passed committee 26-24 on party lines. GOP House majority will likely hold. Senate has local-control skeptics on both sides. Needs a Senate companion or reconciliation path to become law.

stocks.name: American Tower Corp
stocks.reason: Tower REIT directly benefits from streamlined modification approvals.

stocks.name: Crown Castle
stocks.reason: Small cell densification accelerated under eliminated local permitting.

stocks.name: SBA Communications
stocks.reason: Same tower REIT tailwind as AMT/CCI.

stocks.name: AT&T
stocks.reason: Lower rollout cost for 5G dense urban deployments.

stocks.name: T-Mobile
stocks.reason: Rural and suburban small cell deployment friction reduced.

upcomingBills.title: ESA Amendments Act of 2025
upcomingBills.summary: Major overhaul of the Endangered Species Act. Allows state/county government data to qualify as 'best available science,' requires economic impact assessments before listing decisions, limits critical habitat designation on private land with active conservation plans.
upcomingBills.passReasoning: Passed committee 25-16 on partisan lines. House floor vote expected to succeed. Senate ESA reform has stalled historically. Some bipartisan rural-state support possible.

stocks.name: ExxonMobil
stocks.reason: Fewer ESA delays on pipeline and drilling siting reviews.

stocks.name: Freeport-McMoRan
stocks.reason: Copper/gold mining projects less vulnerable to ESA litigation.

stocks.name: NextEra Energy
stocks.reason: Wind/solar siting on federal land becomes easier.

upcomingBills.title: FISA Section 702 Long-Term Reauthorization
upcomingBills.summary: Full multi-year reauthorization of FISA Section 702. Trump wants a clean 18-month or 5-year extension. Privacy hawks in both parties demand warrant requirements before accessing American citizens' communications data.
upcomingBills.passLabel: Coin Flip — Warrant Provision Is the Fault Line
upcomingBills.passReasoning: The same coalition of ~20 GOP privacy hawks + most Democrats blocked the 18-month clean extension. A deal requires warrant reforms Trump accepts, or enough Democratic crossovers to overcome the rebels.

stocks.name: Alphabet / Google
stocks.reason: Clean extension = continued compelled data handover with no new legal clarity.

stocks.name: Meta Platforms
stocks.reason: Same compelled-provider exposure as Google under 702.

stocks.name: Cyber Security ETF
stocks.reason: Any extension keeps national security tech spend flowing.

stocks.name: CACI International
stocks.reason: Intelligence/surveillance IT contractor benefits from program continuation.

pastBills.title: FirstNet Reauthorization Act of 2026
pastBills.summary: Reauthorizes the First Responder Network Authority (FirstNet), the national public safety broadband network used by police, fire, and EMS, operated in partnership with AT&T.
pastBills.passReasoning: Under suspension of the rules. First responder infrastructure has near-universal congressional support. No meaningful opposition expected.

stocks.name: AT&T
stocks.projectedReason: AT&T holds the exclusive FirstNet contract — reauthorization locks in the public safety revenue stream long term.

pastBills.title: Boundary Waters Mining Withdrawal Repeal
pastBills.summary: Used the Congressional Review Act to overturn a 20-year federal mining withdrawal protecting the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota from sulfide-ore copper mining.
pastBills.predictedLabel: Likely to squeak by — CRA bypasses filibuster
pastBills.voteNote: Two Republicans (Collins, Tillis) defected. Hawley absent.

stocks.name: Antofagasta (Twin Metals parent)
stocks.projectedReason: Would unlock the copper mining project blocked since 2021.
stocks.actualNote: Antofagasta shares rose on London exchange. Twin Metals project now legally re-openable.

stocks.name: Freeport-McMoRan
stocks.projectedReason: Precedent for CRA use against land withdrawals benefits copper miners broadly.
stocks.actualNote: Modest sympathy trade on reduced federal mining restriction sentiment.

stocks.name: Newmont Corporation
stocks.projectedReason: Same precedent-setting benefit for extractive industry permitting.
stocks.actualNote: Minor positive reaction; gold/copper miners broadly up.

stocks.name: Vale S.A.
stocks.projectedReason: Mining deregulation sentiment globally positive for major miners.

pastBills.title: FISA Section 702 — 10-Day Extension
pastBills.summary: A stopgap 10-day extension of Section 702 of FISA, extended to April 30 after the full 5-year and 18-month renewals failed due to a bloc of ~20 Republican privacy hawks.
pastBills.predictedLabel: Uncertain — GOP rebels + privacy caucus unpredictable
pastBills.voteNote: 10-day stopgap passed by voice vote in both chambers. The 18-month clean version failed 228-197 before the stopgap was agreed.

stocks.name: Alphabet / Google
stocks.actualNote: Market read the 10-day extension as a delay, not resolution. No directional move.

stocks.name: Meta Platforms
stocks.projectedReason: Section 702 compels platforms to hand over user data without warrants.
stocks.actualNote: Negligible reaction. Market focused on earnings cycle.

stocks.name: T-Mobile
stocks.projectedReason: Carriers are primary compelled providers under 702.
stocks.actualNote: No material move. Short extension duration removed urgency.

stocks.name: ETFMG Prime Cyber Security ETF
stocks.projectedReason: Continued surveillance program = sustained demand for cybersecurity.
stocks.actualNote: Minor tick up on continued national security spend expectations.

pastBills.title: DHS FY2026 Funding Bill
pastBills.summary: Final FY2026 funding for DHS including ICE and Border Patrol through May 22, 2026. Passed amid a 27+ day partial government shutdown. Only 7 Democrats voted yes.
pastBills.predictedLabel: Messy — shutdown dynamics and Dem defections key
pastBills.voteNote: Highly partisan. 7 Democrats crossed over. Some conservative Republicans objected to earmarks.

stocks.name: GEO Group
stocks.projectedReason: Private detention operator directly benefits from ICE/CBP funding.
stocks.actualNote: GEO surged on passage; ICE detention contract certainty restored.

stocks.name: CoreCivic
stocks.projectedReason: Same private detention exposure as GEO.
stocks.actualNote: Direct beneficiary of ICE/CBP funding continuity.

stocks.name: Axon Enterprise
stocks.projectedReason: Law enforcement tech spend rises with DHS funding.
stocks.actualNote: TASER and body cam contracts with DHS components supported.

stocks.name: Booz Allen Hamilton
stocks.projectedReason: DHS consulting and IT contracts resume after shutdown.
stocks.actualNote: Modest move; shutdown end broadly positive for gov contractors.
